identity and verification.description: Questions related to confirming or validating personal information such as name, date of birth, address, or identity for security or record‑matching purposes.

consent and authorization.description: Questions about permissions required to discuss, manage, or access information on behalf of another individual, including authorized representatives or power of attorney.

contact and communication preferences.description: Preferences or updates regarding how communications are delivered, including phone, email, text, language, print format, or consent to future contact.

health status and assessment.description: Questions related to general health information, conditions, medications, providers, or health assessments that are not directly tied to enrollment status.

underwriting and risk questions.description: Questions about how medical history, conditions, treatments, or health disclosures affect eligibility, approval, pricing, or underwriting outcomes.

company verification and legitimacy.description: Questions about the legitimacy, affiliation, or authorization of a company, plan, caller, advertisement, or outreach communication.

complaints and escalation.description: Concerns or issues related to dissatisfaction with service, unresolved problems, repeated contacts, or requests to escalate or file a complaint.

external assistance and social services.description: Requests for information or help involving organizations or programs outside the plan, such as Medicaid, Social Security, utilities, food, housing, or caregiving resources.

call handling and operational flow.description: Questions about call routing, transfers, callbacks, departments, recordings, availability, or difficulty reaching the appropriate team.

non-actionable or unintelligible.description: Statements or questions that are incomplete, unclear, nonsensical, or do not express a specific, actionable intent.

non-english call.description: Interactions primarily conducted in a language other than English, including requests for translation or language‑specific support.
